# All Star Dryer Vent
> Professional dryer vent cleaning, repair, restoration, and inspection in Reno, Sparks, and Northern Nevada since 2013.

## Services and Pricing

### Dryer Vent Cleaning
Typical cost: $89-$149
Includes full lint extraction from the dryer cabinet exhaust port through to the exterior termination, airflow test before and after service, inspection of duct materials and termination cap, and a written safety summary.

### Dryer Vent Repair
Typical cost: $149-$299 depending on materials and scope
Covers replacement of crushed flexible foil duct with code-compliant rigid metal duct, repair or replacement of damaged transitions, resealing of joints and connections, and correction of improper vent routing.

### Dryer Vent Restoration
For severely damaged or fully blocked systems. Includes full system assessment, removal of non-compliant materials, installation of rigid metal duct per IRC and manufacturer specs, new exterior termination cap, and final airflow verification.

### Dryer Vent Inspection
12-point safety audit with camera inspection of the full duct run. Identifies blockages, crushed sections, improper materials, code violations, and exterior termination issues. Recommended annually for homes with pets, large families, or dryers running daily.

## How Dryer Vent Cleaning Works — Step by Step
1. Technician arrives and inspects the exterior vent termination for blockages, damage, or pest intrusion.
2. Airflow is tested at the dryer exhaust port to establish a baseline CFM reading.
3. The dryer is disconnected and the transition hose is inspected for crushing, kinking, or foil material that should be replaced.
4. A high-powered rotary brush system is inserted from the exterior termination and run through the full length of the duct, dislodging lint from the duct walls.
5. A commercial HEPA vacuum captures all extracted lint at the dryer connection point, keeping the work area clean.
6. The duct run is inspected for obstructions, damage, and code compliance.
7. Airflow is re-tested after cleaning. A properly cleaned vent should read 80+ CFM for most residential systems.
8. The technician reconnects the dryer, checks all connections for leaks, and walks the homeowner through findings.
9. A written safety summary is provided, including recommendations if repair or replacement is needed.

## Frequently Asked Questions

How often should I have my dryer vent cleaned?
Most homes need professional dryer vent cleaning every 12-18 months. Households with pets, large families, or dryers running daily should schedule annual service. Longer vent runs (common in two-story homes and ranch-style layouts) accumulate lint faster and may need attention sooner.

What does dryer vent cleaning cost in Reno or Sparks?
Dryer vent cleaning typically costs $89-$149. The final price depends on vent length, termination type (roof vs. wall), and the severity of lint buildup. All Star Dryer Vent provides upfront pricing before beginning any work — no hidden fees.

Is a clogged dryer vent a fire hazard?
Yes. Lint is highly flammable and the leading cause of dryer fires. The U.S. Fire Administration reports nearly 3,000 dryer fires annually in the United States. A fully blocked vent forces heat and combustion gases to back up into the home. Professional cleaning eliminates this risk.

What are the signs of a clogged dryer vent?
- Clothes take two or more full cycles to dry
- The dryer exterior is hot to the touch after a cycle
- A burning or musty smell during operation
- The laundry room feels unusually warm while the dryer runs
- Lint visible around the exterior vent flap or termination cap
- The dryer shuts off mid-cycle due to thermal overload

How long does dryer vent cleaning take?
A standard cleaning takes 45-90 minutes. Longer vent runs, roof terminations, or significant blockages may take longer. Technicians do not rush — airflow is tested after service to confirm the vent is fully clear.

Can I clean the dryer vent myself?
Box-store vent cleaning kits extend up to 12 feet and capture surface lint but cannot reach or dislodge deep blockages in longer runs, bends, or transitions. Professional rotary brush systems reach the full duct length. DIY cleaning also does not include a safety inspection for damaged ductwork, improper materials, or code violations.

What is the difference between dryer vent cleaning and dryer vent repair?
Cleaning removes lint buildup from an intact, properly installed vent system. Repair addresses physical damage — crushed flexible duct, disconnected joints, improper routing, or non-compliant materials. After a cleaning inspection, if repair is needed, All Star Dryer Vent provides a quote before any additional work is performed.

How do I know if my dryer vent needs repair vs. cleaning?
A cleaning inspection will identify whether the system needs repair. Common repair triggers include: flexible foil duct (a fire code violation in many municipalities), duct lengths exceeding 25 feet without offsets, joints that have separated inside a wall, and exterior caps that are stuck shut or missing the flap.

Do you serve areas outside Reno and Sparks?
Yes. All Star Dryer Vent serves Reno, Sparks, Incline Village, Minden, Cold Springs, Hidden Valley, Lockwood, Sun Valley, and Washoe Valley in Northern Nevada.
